title: Journal sequence collision when non-interactive breakpoints auto-approve back-to-back
summary: Fix the SDK journal append path so rapid or concurrent PROCESS_LOG writes, especially from consecutive non-interactive breakpoint auto-approvals, cannot allocate duplicate numeric journal sequence numbers and cannot cause a phantom RUN_FAILED after RUN_COMPLETED.
implementationFocus: Read issue #191 and duplicate tracker #120 before changing code.
Trace the current branch for existing partial fixes around PROCESS_LOG logSeq and non-interactive breakpoint auto-approval.
Treat appendEvent sequence allocation as the primary race boundary: reading existing sequence numbers, selecting max+1, and writing the journal file must be serialized per run directory.
Prefer a localized per-run in-memory mutex or promise queue around appendEvent unless the trace proves cross-process writes are in scope for this issue.
Preserve journal filename format, checksums, SDK version stamping, harness/session stamping, loadJournal ordering, and EffectIndex strict sequence validation.
Do not use duplicate-sequence forgiveness in replay as the main fix unless a maintainer explicitly approves that semantic change.
Preserve existing non-interactive breakpoint logSeq behavior if it is already present; adjust only if needed to satisfy #120/#191 together.
acceptanceFocus: Concurrent appendEvent calls for the same run directory produce contiguous unique sequence numbers.
A minimal process with two consecutive ctx.breakpoint calls in --non-interactive mode completes successfully.
A subsequent run:status or run:iterate after completion does not append a phantom RUN_FAILED after RUN_COMPLETED.
PROCESS_LOG events from auto-approved breakpoints retain distinct replay-deduplication metadata where applicable.
EffectIndex continues to reject genuinely corrupt journals with skipped, duplicate, regressing, or out-of-order sequences.
The fix does not introduce arbitrary sleeps or timing-sensitive tests.
The fix remains local to SDK journal/runtime behavior and does not convert the process to shell subtasks.
qualityGates: Regression tests are written before implementation and fail against the un-serialized append allocator or document an existing partial fix that already covers the symptom.
Targeted storage tests cover concurrent appendEvent calls and verify unique contiguous sequence numbers.
Targeted breakpoint/runtime tests cover back-to-back non-interactive breakpoint auto-approvals and PROCESS_LOG metadata.
Replay/status verification confirms completed runs remain completed and no spurious RUN_FAILED is appended.
EffectIndex corruption tests still pass and strict validation is not weakened.
npm exec --yes --package=vitest -- vitest run --config vitest.config.ts packages/sdk/src/storage/__tests__/storage.test.ts packages/sdk/src/runtime/intrinsics/__tests__/breakpointSkip.test.ts passes.

npm exec --yes --package=vitest -- vitest run --config vitest.config.ts packages/sdk/src/runtime/__tests__/effectIndex.test.ts packages/sdk/src/runtime/__tests__/orchestrateIteration.integration.test.ts passes.
npm run test:sdk passes or unrelated pre-existing failures are documented with evidence.
Compatibility review approves journal append serialization, replay determinism, and run-lock interaction.
nonGoals: Do not implement broad replay-time renumbering as a substitute for preventing duplicate sequence allocation.
Do not weaken EffectIndex sequence validation to hide journal corruption.
Do not redesign the journal format or replace numeric sequence prefixes with ULID-only ordering in this fix.
Do not add shell subtasks to this Babysitter process.
Do not modify source files during planning; implementation belongs to the execution of this process.
notes: .a5c/process-library/ was not present while authoring; matching methodologies and specializations were researched under library/ and repo-specific policy under docs/agent-reference/process-authoring.md.

The current branch already shows PROCESS_LOG logSeq-related code in packages/sdk/src/runtime/intrinsics/breakpoint.ts and tests in packages/sdk/src/runtime/intrinsics/__tests__/breakpointSkip.test.ts; the execution process must verify whether that is sufficient for duplicate tracker #120 before changing it.

The current appendEvent implementation in packages/sdk/src/storage/journal.ts computes max(existing seq)+1 before writeFileAtomic without an append-level critical section; this is the central race to investigate.
